what_it_does: This file is a project-instruction and convention document for an AI assistant working on the episodic-memory Claude Code plugin; it documents architecture, build/test/release workflow, version management, critical runtime invariants, and the source file layout.
observations: TypeScript source lives in src/ and is compiled to dist/ by tsc && esbuild; both directories are committed.
The MCP server is dist/mcp-server.js, launched by cli/mcp-server-wrapper.js from the plugin manifest.
src/version.ts is generated from package.json and is gitignored; it must never be hand-edited.
Version must stay in lockstep across package.json, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, and an external ../superpowers-marketplace registry.
Release engineering is an eight-step checklist covering tests, build, version bump, changelog, commit/tag, GitHub release, marketplace registry update, and smoke test.
A summarizer recursion guard is implemented via EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD and shouldSkipReentrantSync().
Embedding changes require bumping EMBEDDING_VERSION to trigger automatic re-embedding of stale rows under a lock file.
dist/ is committed and must be rebuilt from src/ after source changes; hand-edits are clobbered.
Tests use mkdtempSync and per-test environment variables to avoid touching real user config directories.
The file layout section maps each src/ module to a responsibility such as embeddings, search, indexer, sync, summarizer, db, paths, parser, and MCP server.
standouts: The external marketplace registry update is called out as the load-bearing step that is easy to forget.
Recursion/reentrancy guards are treated as a critical safety invariant across subprocess boundaries.
Embedding pipeline changes must bump a version constant to avoid silent search-quality degradation.
The version pipeline (package.json -> generate-version.js -> src/version.ts -> MCP server identity) is explicitly protected from hardcoding.
conventions: Follows a conventional project README/instructions structure with quick orientation, build commands, and file layout.
Mandates a generated-file workflow where a build hook regenerates src/version.ts.
Uses environment-variable guards to prevent recursive subprocess behavior.
Requires lockstep versioning across multiple JSON files and an external repository.
Treats tests as the executable specification for load-bearing invariants.
concerns: The release depends on an external repository outside this repo, which is easy to forget and hard to validate locally.
Committed dist/ can drift if a contributor edits src/ without running npm run build.
Version is scattered across three local files plus one external file, increasing bump risk.
The eight-step release checklist is manual and therefore error-prone.
notes: This is the onboarding and release-playbook document that encodes how to safely build, test, version, and ship the episodic-memory plugin without missing load-bearing guards or registry updates.
questions: What is the exact schema of .version-bump.json?
How is access and authorization managed for the ../superpowers-marketplace repository?
What triggers src/version.ts regeneration outside the prebuild and pretest hooks?
What does 'MCP server identity' encompass beyond the version field?
How are dist/ merge conflicts handled when multiple branches rebuild it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content, though the file also functions as a detailed release runbook and operational-safety manual.
apparent_themes: Safety through explicit guards and locks
Avoidance of silent behavioral drift in embeddings
intention_signals: The document appears designed to encode institutional knowledge so an AI assistant can safely modify, build, test, and release the episodic-memory plugin without overlooking critical invariants like recursion-guard propagation, embedding-version bumps, marketplace registry updates, or dist/ rebuilds.
novelty: It is unusual for a project file to explicitly instruct an AI assistant on conventions, and to detail a release process that includes an external marketplace registry update plus a cross-process recursion guard using environment variables.
contribution: This file contributes a single source of onboarding and release guidance that preserves critical invariants (reentrancy guard, embedding migration, version pipeline, committed dist/) and reduces the chance that load-bearing steps are forgotten during AI-assisted development.
summary_for_embedding: Project instructions and release runbook for the episodic-memory Claude Code plugin, covering source layout, build/test workflow, version management, MCP server launch, recursion-guard and embedding-migration invariants, and an eight-step release process including an external marketplace registry update.
intention_for_embedding: To enable an AI assistant to safely navigate, modify, build, test, and release the episodic-memory codebase while preserving its load-bearing safety and versioning invariants.
